violations.text: Soap and paper towel was not available at the hand-washing sink in the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent dropping at bar and basement areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PCS has completed an aggressive treatment and is presently treating twice per month. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026
The condensate from the HVAC system draining into the 3-bay sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

The following was observed in poor repair: -dining room floor (buckled) -missing floor tiles (ice machine room) -roof of exterior storage shed (plastic roof pieces missing). Repeat Violation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Wiping cloths, dish soap, and other items were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Several food items were observed held at 44-52 degrees Fahrenheit on ice. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff submerged containers in ice during inspection. Salmon was disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below. There is insufficient hot holding equipment to maintain foods at 135 degrees F or above. PIC stated a prep unit has been ordered and delivery expected 3/6/2026. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2026
ROP salmon was observed thawed with packaging intact. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The following were observed in poor repair: -dining room floor (buckled) -floor tile missing in ice machine room -roof of exterior storage shed (plastic roof pieces missing). New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2026

Wiping cloths, equipment and other items were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Staff removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Staff provided paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Machine taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; ice disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026
A sheet pan with food debris was observed stored on a table in the outdoor storage shed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by deceased rodents, rodent droppings, nesting materials, and water sources in basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026
Two deceased rodents were observed in traps in the basement. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the black rolling cart have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Per PIC interview, the HVAC/mini-split system is in poor repair, with leaks in multiple areas of the facility. Inspector observed water damage to floor in dining room and water pooling under HVAC/mini-split PVC pipes in basement; PIC stated this was believed to be due to these system leaks. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The following were observed in poor repair: -dining room floor (buckling, delaminating) -roof of exterior storage shed (plastic roof pieces missing) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2026

The floors and shelving units in the basement have an accumulation of rodent droppings and water debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
Menu is lacking an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026

Various beef dishes are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by inspector observing a live mouse. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigeration gaskets, refrigeration shelving, ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026
A leak was observed in a drainpipe in the basement. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026
A hole was observed in the wall in the kitchen. Staff filled hole with steel wool during inspection. A hole was observed in the ceiling tile near ice machine. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026

The floors under the bar and in the basement have an accumulation of debris and/or rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Staff began cleaning bar area during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement and rear shed areas. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2026

The handwashing sink in the kitchen was observed in poor repair, missing faucet handle and leaking when service turned on. PIC stated plumber scheduled to return today. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service to be thoroughly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Wood is used in the construction of (bar) counter and wall in kitchen. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Owner informed to commercially seal all areas. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The walls in the (bar) warewashing area are constructed of (painted stucco). This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2025
